articles.title: From 'BuddhaBot' to $1.99 chats with AI Jesus, the faith-based tech boom is here
articles.description: For some evangelical Christians, faith is about having a personal relationship with Jesus. At $1.99 per minute, the tech company Just Like Me is taking that concept to a new level. Users of the platform can join video calls with an avatar of Jesus generated by artificial intelligence. articles.title: UK regulators rushing to assess risks of latest Anthropic AI model: report
articles.description: British financial regulators are holding urgent talks with the government's cyber security agency and major banks to assess risks posed by the latest artificial intelligence model from Anthropic, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.
articles.content:

articles.title: China’s AI boom: blazing IPOs, an AI agent craze, and a new ‘token economy'
articles.description: China hopes to build a “token economy,” backed by open-source models and real-world AI applications—even as U.S. export controls still hold things back.

articles.title: 'Stop hiring humans'? Silicon Valley confronts AI job panic
articles.description: AI industry insiders want workers to code smarter, think harder and lean into their humanity -- but still dodge the question of how many jobs artificial intelligence will destroy. articles.title: Durand Police Department experiments with Code Four AI for writing reports
articles.description: For the last month, the Durand Police Department has been using Code Four, an AI assistant, to generate their police reports.
articles.content:
DURAND, Wis. (WEAU) - For the last month, the Durand Police Department has been using Code Four, an AI assistant, to generate their police reports.
This program analyzes body camera footage to write the reports while officers and supervisors review t... [641 chars]

articles.title: Apple is reportedly testing four different designs for its smart glasses.
articles.description:
The next big product category Apple is supposedly tackling is display-free smart glasses to rival Meta’s. But the design has yet to be locked in, according to Cupertino whisperer Mark Gurman. The company might launch some or all four of the designs under consideration:
> A large rectangular frame, reminiscent of Ray-Ban Wayfarers
> A slimmer rectangular design, similar to the glasses worn by Apple Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook
> Larger oval or circular frames
> A smaller, more refined oval or circular option
[Link: Apple AI Glasses Will Rival Meta’s With Several Styles, Oval Cameras | https://www.bloomberg.com/news/newsletters/2026-04-12/apple-ai-smart-glasses-features-styles-colors-cameras-giannandrea-leaving-mnvtz4yg?srnd=undefined | Bloomberg]
articles.content:
